content.fact: Bakugo's criticism about special treatment helps Deku realize that Ochaco occupies a distinct emotional place for him.
content.injection: Use Bakugo's 'special treatment' pressure as a catalyst for Deku recognizing Ochaco as specifically important, not merely one cherished person among everyone he respects.

entries.title: Deku And Ochaco Are Not Automatically Married

context.label: Deku and Ochaco choose to speak honestly

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia Chapter 431 'More' from Volume 42. Optional post-series extra overlay after Chapter 430; not loaded by default for the Chapter 430 epilogue deck.

content.fact: Chapter 431 moves Deku and Ochaco toward a relationship, but it does not require scenes to treat them as married, cohabiting, or long-established partners.
content.injection: When the More overlay is active, Deku and Ochaco can be beginning a clearer mutual relationship. Do not jump automatically to marriage, children, or a fully defined long-term status unless the user story adds it.

entries.title: Toga Memory Should Not Become Literal Possession

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia Chapter 431 'More' from Volume 42. Optional post-series extra overlay after Chapter 430; not loaded by default for the Chapter 430 epilogue deck.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded unless added as separate overlay decks. Chapter 431 material should not leak into earlier school-era, war, or Chapter 430 scenes unless this overlay is active.

content.fact: Ochaco wonders about Himiko's lingering presence through memory and blood, but Chapter 431 must not be treated as proof that Toga literally possesses Ochaco or survives as an active Quirk-vestige entity.
content.injection: Keep Toga's presence ambiguous and emotional. Avoid literal possession, resurrection, or a new supernatural mechanic unless the story intentionally diverges.

content.fact: Deku and Bakugo interact as adults whose rivalry, criticism, and care have matured into peer-level tension rather than childhood bullying or simple hero worship.

entries.title: Adult Class Banter Should Carry Separate Lives

content.fact: The former classmates joke, tease, and compare adult statuses from the position of people who no longer live the same daily school routine.
content.injection: Write the reunion with easy history and mild awkward distance: they know each other deeply but now have separate agencies, careers, schedules, and adult concerns.

entries.title: Shigaraki's Last Words Still Press On Deku

content.fact: During Chapter 431, Deku reflects on Shigaraki's final words and on whether there are parts of himself he has not yet discovered.
content.injection: Use Shigaraki's memory as a quiet pressure on Deku's adult self-understanding, not as a literal haunting or active villain return.
